methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $10.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.8B - $15.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Ayar Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $3.75B

data.narrative:
Crusoe Energy, a Series E stage AI & Software company founded by Chase Lochmiller and Cully Cavness, has raised $1.375B to date against a current post-money valuation of $10B+. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR), including same-sector and adjacent-stage companies such as Applied Intuition, Wayve, and Ayar Labs.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical for Series E advancement, projects a valuation of $14.0B from the $10.0B base. Peer-median methodology across 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields $8.6B, with the peer set ranging from $3.8B (Ayar Labs, Series E) to $15.0B (Applied Intuition, Series F). 

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $7.3B (low) to $16.1B (high), with a median of $11.3B. The current $10B+ valuation positions the company near the lower-median bound of this range, suggesting modest upside under stage-progression mechanics but potential compression risk if peer growth rates moderate. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three-company peer median and whether the 1.4x multiplier remains calibrated for late-stage software funding conditions.
